skill.name: codebase-cleanup-refactor-clean
skill.description: Guides practical codebase cleanup using clean code principles, SOLID design, focused refactoring plans, and verification steps.
skill.summary: Plan and execute incremental refactors that improve readability, maintainability, and test coverage.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported a very high score, but review found the hits are documentation examples, Markdown code fences, inline resource references, and prose. No executable hooks, hidden instructions, credential collection, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempts were found in SKILL.md or the referenced playbook.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Examples Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to fenced code examples and inline Markdown references. They are instructional text, not executable skill code or shell invocation logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown fences, language examples, or resource links. No runtime script or command wrapper is present in the scanned files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network and File Access in Refactoring Examples
low_findings.description: The email, sendmail, and file write patterns appear inside a before-and-after refactoring scenario. They illustrate legacy code being cleaned up and are not invoked by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The SMTP and file write references are inside a fenced Python example. The skill contains no tool code that sends network traffic or writes files automatically.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance and Weak Crypto Terms in Prose
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts match ordinary words in prose, interfaces, metrics, and checklists. They do not describe malicious discovery or cryptographic behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are checklist items, code quality metrics, or harmless example identifiers. No evidence of system probing, hidden file harvesting, or weak cryptographic implementation was found.

content.user_title: Refactor Codebases with Clean Code Practices
content.value_statement: Messy code slows reviews and raises change risk. This skill guides incremental refactoring, cleaner design, and focused verification.
actual_capabilities: Identifies code smells, duplication, oversized functions, and maintainability risks.
Builds prioritized refactoring plans with impact, effort, and risk notes.
Applies clean code and SOLID principles to restructure code incrementally.
Suggests focused unit tests, regression checks, and quality metrics.
Prepares before-and-after explanations for reviewable cleanup work.
limitations: It cannot guarantee behavior preservation without tests or runnable examples.
Large architecture changes still require human scope and design approval.
It does not replace security review for production code changes.
Project-specific conventions must be provided when they are important.

use_cases.title: Prepare Legacy Code for Feature Work
use_cases.description: Create a staged cleanup plan before adding new behavior to a fragile module.

use_cases.title: Improve Pull Request Quality
use_cases.description: Refactor duplicated or complex code into smaller, testable units before review.

use_cases.title: Reduce Technical Debt Safely
use_cases.description: Rank cleanup opportunities by business value, risk, and available test coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Find Cleanup Targets
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner audit of a small module
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this file for code smells, duplication, naming issues, and risky complexity. Return a prioritized cleanup plan with test suggestions.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor a Function Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this function for readability while preserving behavior. Explain each change and list the tests needed to verify it.

prompt_templates.title: Apply SOLID Principles
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this class for SOLID violations. Propose a smaller design, then implement the lowest-risk refactor first.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Codebase Cleanup
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this codebase for high-impact refactoring work. Rank changes by value, effort, risk, dependencies, and verification strategy.

output_examples.input: A long order-processing module mixes validation, persistence, notification, and logging.
output: Separates validation, repository access, notification, and logging into clearer responsibilities.
Adds a staged refactor plan that starts with tests and low-risk extraction work.
Reports expected improvements in readability, testability, and review size.

output_examples.input: A pull request contains duplicated conditionals and unclear names across several functions.
output: Highlights duplicate logic and confusing names with specific refactor targets.
Recommends extracted helpers, clearer domain terms, and regression checks.
Keeps changes small enough for normal code review.

output_examples.input: A team wants a quarterly technical debt plan for a service with weak tests.
output: Ranks debt by production risk, change frequency, test gaps, and business impact.
Suggests first adding characterization tests around fragile behavior.
Defines measurable outcomes for complexity, coverage, and module boundaries.

best_practices: Start with tests or characterization checks before changing behavior-sensitive code.
Keep refactors small, reviewable, and tied to a clear quality goal.
Measure impact with complexity, duplication, coverage, and defect risk indicators.
anti_patterns: Do not rewrite large subsystems without an agreed migration plan.
Do not mix behavior changes with cleanup unless the risk is explicit.
Do not apply design patterns when simpler names or extraction solve the problem.

faq.question: Can this skill refactor an entire repository?
faq.answer: Yes, but it should work in stages. Start with analysis, priorities, and tests before large edits.

faq.question: Does it change code automatically?
faq.answer: It can guide changes when used with a coding agent. Review and test all edits before merging.

faq.question: What languages does it support?
faq.answer: It gives general refactoring guidance and includes examples for Python, TypeScript, Java, Go, JavaScript, and YAML.

faq.question: Can it improve test coverage?
faq.answer: It can identify test gaps and suggest focused unit, regression, and characterization tests.

faq.question: Is it useful for security cleanup?
faq.answer: It can flag risky patterns during refactoring, but it is not a replacement for dedicated security testing.

faq.question: How should I scope a refactor request?
faq.answer: Provide the target files, current pain points, test commands, and constraints for review size or release timing.
